title: Warm up
content: Introduces three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—through illustrated images of taxi drivers viewing each city from inside a car. This sets the stage for comparing the cities' distinct characteristics.
key_claims: Beijing is depicted with modern skyscrapers, traffic, and traditional architecture alongside technology (glasses, dashcam).
Chongqing is shown with a driver reading a book against a backdrop of mountainside buildings and a cable car.
Hangzhou is depicted with a driver using a smartphone with AI/robot interface elements near a lake and pagoda.

title: An Evolving Nation
content: This is a title slide introducing the presentation's theme, 'A Ride Through China,' using images of a taxi sign and iconic Chinese architecture from different cities.
key_claims: The presentation theme is 'An Evolving Nation: A Ride Through China'.
Visuals include a taxi sign and traditional/modern buildings representing Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou.

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: Outlines the first session's structure, which involves researching cities through a three-step process: identifying local features, understanding development strategies, and analyzing outcomes and positive impacts. An isometric cityscape illustration accompanies the flow.
key_claims: The research process follows three steps: Local Features → Development Strategies → Outcomes & Positive Impact.
Session 1 focuses on investigation and research of cities.

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: A matching activity slide where students connect three cities (Beijing, Chongqing, Hangzhou) to their corresponding development strategies. Images of Beijing's Forbidden City, Chongqing's illuminated buildings, and Hangzhou's pagoda accompany the exercise.
key_claims: Beijing is associated with Technology & tradition integration (融合).
Chongqing is associated with Sustainable urban development.
Hangzhou is associated with Tourism & Economic growth.

title: Session 2 Ask & Analyse: critical analysis & comparative insights
content: Describes the task instructions for Session 2, dividing students into two roles: 'city observers' who select and ask questions, and 'city experts' who answer using text examples. This promotes critical analysis and comparison between cities.
key_claims: City observers select 1 question from a question list to ask each group.
City experts answer the question using text examples.

title: Session 3 Promote Your City!
content: Details Session 3, where each group acts as 'City Promotion Ambassadors' (城市宣传大使) to create a 2-minute promotional speech for their city, incorporating a taxi driver's story and city experts' strategic explanations. Specific requirements include using key phrases and creating a slogan.
key_claims: Each group must create a 2-minute promotional speech as City Promotion Ambassadors.
The taxi driver shares a fun story about the city; city experts explain strategies.
Requirement 1: Use at least 3 key phrases from the text.
Requirement 2: End with a creative slogan.

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: Summarizes the three cities' development strategies as seen through taxi drivers' perspectives, reinforcing the concept that China is an evolving nation with unique, locally-tailored development strategies for each city.
key_claims: Beijing represents technology & tradition integration.
Chongqing represents sustainable urban development.
Hangzhou represents tourism & economic growth.
Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote city development (因地制宜).
